Title: Successful user registration with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- Firebase/Firestore is accessible.
- No existing user with the provided email.
Test Steps:
1. User enters registration data (email, password, username) via UI.
2. UI sends `creaAccount(email, password)` to Firebase/Authentication.
3. Firebase/Authentication validates data (format, uniqueness).
4. Firestore confirms data validity and returns a token.
5. UI saves user data (`nome`, `email`, `token`) to the system.
6. UI displays confirmation: *"Registration successful!"*
Expected Result:
- User account created in Firestore with `id`, `username`, `email`, and `password` (hashed).
- Token returned to UI for future authentication.
- No error messages displayed.

Title: Failed user registration with invalid email format
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid email and submits registration.
2. UI sends `creaAccount(invalid.email, password)` to Firebase/Authentication.
3. Firebase/Authentication rejects data due to invalid email format.
4. Firestore returns validation failure.
5. UI displays error: *"Invalid email format. Please correct and try again."*
Expected Result:
- No new user record created in Firestore.
- Error message shown without exposing validation details.
- User can retry with corrected data.

Title: Successful user login with correct credentials
Preconditions: - User `test.user@example.com` exists in Firestore with password `SecurePass123!`.
Test Steps:
1. User enters email and password via UI.
2. UI sends `sign(email, password)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication validates credentials.
4. FirebaseAuthentication returns a valid token to UI.
5. FirebaseFirestore retrieves user data using the token’s UID.
6. UI displays: *"Login successful!"*
Expected Result:
- Token stored locally for authenticated sessions.
- User session initialized with loaded user data (`id`, `username`, `email`).
- No error messages.

Title: Failed user login with incorrect password
Test Steps:
1. User enters correct email but wrong password.
2. UI sends `sign(email, WrongPass123!)` to FirebaseAuthentication.
3. FirebaseAuthentication rejects credentials.
4. UI displays: *"Error: Invalid password. Please try again."*
Expected Result:
- No token issued.
- No user data loaded.
- Error message shown; user can retry.

Title: Create a new entity linked to an area
Preconditions:
- Authenticated user with role allowing entity creation.
- Area "Downtown" exists with `id=1`.
Test Steps:
1. Authenticated user calls `Entities.create(args)` with entity data.
2. System validates `name`, `description`, `type`, and `area_id`.
3. System associates entity with area `id=1` ("Downtown").
4. System persists entity to database.
5. System returns success confirmation.
Expected Result:
- New entity record created with `id=101`, linked to `area_id=1`.
- No validation errors (e.g., duplicate `name` or invalid `area_id`).
- Notification sent to user: *"Entity 'Police Station' created successfully."*

Title: Create entity with invalid area reference
Preconditions:
- Authenticated user.
- No area exists with `id=999`.
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to create entity with non-existent `area_id`.
2. System validates `area_id` against database.
3. System detects invalid `area_id` and rejects creation.
4. System returns error: *"Area not found. Please select a valid area."*
Expected Result:
- No new entity record created.
- Error message displayed to user.
- User prompted to select an existing area.

Title: Successful report creation with image upload
Preconditions:
- Authenticated user `id=1`.
- Entity `id=101` ("Police Station") exists in area `id=1`.
- FirebaseStorage is accessible.
Test Data:
- Report: `user_id=1`, `area_id=1`, `date=2023-11-15T14:30:00`, `content="Broken streetlight at Main St"`, `urgenza="High"`, `image="streetlight.jpg"`
- Coordinates: `40.7128, -74.0060`
Test Steps:
1. User submits report via UI with description, image, coordinates, and urgency.
2. UI validates data completeness (all fields filled).
3. UI uploads `streetlight.jpg` to FirebaseStorage.
4. FirebaseStorage returns image URL.
5. Firebase searches for nearest entity (`id=101`) in area `id=1`.
6. System creates report record with `entity_id=101` and image URL.
7. UI confirms: *"Report sent to Police Station. Urgency: High."*
Expected Result:
- Report record created with `id`, `user_id`, `entity_id`, `date`, `content`, and `image_url`.
- Notification sent to user: *"Your report has been assigned to Police Station."*
- Image stored in FirebaseStorage with accessible URL.

Title: Report creation with incomplete data (missing image)
Test Data: - Report: `content="Pothole on Oak Ave"`, `urgenza="Medium"` (no image, no coordinates)
Test Steps:
1. User submits report without image or coordinates.
2. UI detects missing required fields (`image`, `coordinates`).
3. UI displays: *"Error: Please add an image and location coordinates."*
4. User corrects and resubmits.
5. System processes complete report.
Expected Result:
- Initial submission rejected; no report created.
- Error message highlights missing fields.
- User can retry after providing all data.

Title: System sends notification for successful user registration
Test Steps:
1. System detects `user_registered_successfully = true`.
2. System creates notification record:
   - `user_id=1` (new user),
   - `message="Welcome! Your account has been created."`,
   - `timestamp=current_datetime`.
3. Notification is queued for delivery to the user.
Expected Result:
- New `Notifications` record with `id`, `user_id`, `message`, and `timestamp`.
- User receives notification in their account dashboard or via email (if configured).

Title: System sends error notification for failed login
Preconditions: - User attempts login with incorrect password (see TC-AUTHENTICATION-004).
Test Steps:
1. System detects `user_login_failed = true`.
2. System creates notification record:
   - `user_id=1`,
   - `message="Login failed. Check your credentials."`,
   - `timestamp=current_datetime`.
3. Notification is displayed in UI error modal.
Expected Result:
- Notification record created in `Notifications` table.
- Error message shown to user: *"Invalid password. Please try again."*
- No sensitive data (e.g., correct password) leaked in notification.

Title: Create a new area with valid data
Test Steps:
1. Admin calls `Areas.create(args)` with area data.
2. System validates `name` and `description` (non-empty).
3. System persists area to database.
4. System returns success confirmation.
Expected Result:
- New area record created with `id=2`, `name="Uptown"`, `description="Commercial district"`.
- No validation errors (e.g., duplicate `name`).
- Admin receives notification: *"Area 'Uptown' created successfully."*

Title: Create area with duplicate name
Test Steps:
1. Admin attempts to create area with duplicate `name`.
2. System detects duplicate and rejects creation.
3. System returns error: *"Area name already exists. Choose a unique name."*
Expected Result:
- No new area record created.
- Error message displayed to admin.
- Admin prompted to use a different name.

Title: End-to-end flow: User reports issue → Entity assigned → Notification sent
Preconditions:
- Authenticated user `id=1`.
- Entity `id=101` ("Police Station") exists in area `id=1`.
- FirebaseStorage and Firestore are operational.
Test Steps:
1. User submits report via UI:
   - Description: *"Car accident on Maple Ave"*
   - Image: `accident.jpg`
   - Coordinates: `40.7128, -74.0060`
   - Urgency: *"High"*
2. UI validates data and uploads image to FirebaseStorage.
3. System assigns nearest entity (`id=101`) to report.
4. System creates `Reports` record with `entity_id=101`.
5. System generates notification for user:
   - `message="Your report has been assigned to Police Station. ETA: 10 mins."`
6. User receives confirmation in UI.
Expected Result:
- Report record created with `id`, `user_id`, `entity_id`, `content`, `image_url`, and `date`.
- Image stored in FirebaseStorage with accessible URL.
- Notification record created in `Notifications` for the user.
- UI displays: *"Report sent to Police Station. Tracking #REP-1001."*

Title: Report creation with maximum allowed content length
Preconditions:
- Authenticated user.
- System enforces `content` max length of 1000 characters.
Test Data: - Report `content`: A string of exactly 1000 characters (e.g., repeated `"a"`).
Test Steps:
1. User submits report with 1000-character description.
2. UI validates content length
